news.headline: RaySearch Laboratories AB announces RayStation used for proton arc therapy at Trento Proton Therapy Center in Italy.
news.summary: A company based in Stockholm, RaySearch Laboratories AB (publ), disclosed that they have successfully implemented discrete proton arc optimization in RayStation software for treating patients at the Trento Proton Therapy Center in Italy. This advanced method, in combination with conventional delivery techniques and treatment procedures, is now being utilized for patient treatments. This development signifies a significant milestone in the field of proton therapy and showcases the increasing adoption of innovative technologies in medical facilities.

news.headline: Centric Software releases Centric AI Fashion Inspiration tool for small and medium brands; accelerates design process.
news.summary: Centric Software® introduces Centric AI Fashion Inspiration™ for small and medium brands on December 6, 2024. The innovative tool aims to boost creativity and idea generation for fashion retailers and brands. This release is set to provide accelerated ideation options for emerging businesses in the industry.

news.headline: Explore Fairbanks releases 2025 Visitors Guide, a 72-page publication showcasing Fairbanks and Denali with local attractions.
news.summary: The 2025 Official Fairbanks Visitors Guide has been unveiled by Explore Fairbanks, a detailed 72-page booklet showcasing a wide range of activities, attractions, and local offerings in Fairbanks and nearby areas such as Denali, the Arctic, and Interior Alaska. The guide serves as a go-to resource for visitors seeking information on things to do throughout the year in the region. Fairbanks, Alaska is the focal point of this comprehensive guide, providing insights into the diverse experiences travelers can enjoy while exploring the region and its surroundings.

news.headline: Weed scientists from 3 WSSA affiliates to share new research findings at upcoming meetings in Dec and Jan. Keynote speakers featured.
news.summary: In December and January, three regional branches of the Weed Science Society of America (WSSA) are convening to exchange the latest research discoveries. These meetings will showcase keynote presentations, educational symposia, and numerous chances for networking. Taking place in WESTMINSTER, Colo., starting December 6, 2024, these gatherings are expected to draw a significant number of participants keen on deepening their knowledge in the field.

news.headline: Commercial real estate firm M.C. Layman, founded by Kate Layman, launches in Sept 2024, offers innovative solutions.
news.summary: In December 6, 2024, it was reported that a new full-service commercial real estate company called M.C. Layman was founded in Frederick, Maryland. The company, launched in September of the same year, aims to provide innovative solutions and prioritize client needs. The firm was established by Kate Layman, a well-experienced professional in the industry.

news.headline: Max Powers named President and COO at CreatorIQ, driving customer success and global growth. Los Angeles, Dec 6, 2024.
news.summary: Max Powers has been appointed as President of CreatorIQ, in addition to her current position as Chief Operating Officer. The company aims to drive customer success and global enterprise growth through customer-centric innovation. The announcement was made in Los Angeles on December 6, 2024. This strategic move is focused on enhancing the company's commitment to its customers and facilitating expansion in the global market.

news.headline: ASHA urges steps to reduce stigma against people with communication disorders during holidays, impacting relationships.
news.summary: Adults experiencing challenges with speech, language, and hearing have shared that they encounter stigmatization which adversely affects significant relationships and creates anxiety in social situations. The report was disclosed in Rockville, Maryland on December 6, 2024. Individuals expressed a desire for greater understanding during instances when their communication struggles, with precise statistics cited.

news.headline: E-SoftSys Group celebrates 25 years of innovation in self-storage management software. Based in BLUE BELL, Pa., comprising E-SoftSys LLC, Self Storage Manager, Inc., and Global E-SoftSys Pvt. Ltd. - PRNewswire-PRWeb.
news.summary: A prominent company specializing in Management Software for the Self-Storage, Flea Markets, and Antique Malls sector based in BLUE BELL, Pennsylvania, marked a significant milestone on December 6, 2024. E-SoftSys Group of Companies, which includes E-SoftSys LLC, Self Storage Manager, Inc., and Global E-SoftSys Pvt. Ltd., commemorated a noteworthy achievement.

news.headline: Prima receives $42.5M funding to revolutionize manufacturing in North America.180+ companies use Prima as their sole seller.
news.summary: Prima is focused on improving the dependability and effectiveness of manufacturing procurement. Over 150 top companies in North America have chosen Prima as their exclusive seller of record. In Mexico City on December 6, 2024, Prima, a technology-driven manufacturing company, unveiled its plans to revolutionize the procurement process.

news.headline: Hardin-Simmons student Adaeze Ozuzu receives $2,500 STEM scholarship from SBB Research Group Foundation.
news.summary: Adaeze Ozuzu has been selected as a recipient of the STEM Scholarship by the SBB Research Group Foundation in Chicago on December 6, 2024. This scholarship awards $2,500 to students, aiming to enable them to contribute positively to society through their academic pursuits.

news.headline: Children's Museum of Indianapolis to introduce 110-foot Centennial Ferris Wheel in 2025 for its 100th birthday.
news.summary: In honor of its centennial milestone, The Children's Museum of Indianapolis is set to unveil an impressive Centennial Ferris Wheel standing at a remarkable height of 110 feet. Scheduled for debut on March 15, 2025, this iconic attraction marks a significant moment in the museum's 100th year celebration.

news.headline: 11th year of RISE Flag Football in Orlando area led by Amber and Trevor Anderson, strengthening community and youth sports.
news.summary: Amber and Trevor Anderson are dedicated to enhancing the community and youth sports in Ocoee and Dr. Phillips. RISE Flag Football Powered by Under Armour, spearheaded by the entrepreneurial duo, is now in its 11th year. The initiative aims to promote sports and community engagement.

news.headline: Seniors Helping Seniors® expands to Pittsburgh Northeast led by locals Travis Hudson and Dina DiFranceco.
news.summary: Travis Hudson and Dina DiFranceco, seasoned members of the Pittsburgh community, lead Seniors Helping Seniors®, which provides in-home senior care services in the area. This initiative offers compassionate support from peers to seniors, aiming to address the needs of the elderly population. Established in PITTSBURGH, the organization has gained recognition for its dedication to providing personalized care. This commitment is exemplified through their effective in-home care services that cater to the specific needs of seniors.

news.headline: Lexar introduced two portable SSDs that are 9.5 times faster than hard drives, with speeds up to 1050MB/s read and 1000MB/s write, boosting productivity.
news.summary: SAN JOSE, California - On December 6, 2024, a new technology was introduced that boosts productivity significantly by being over 9 times faster than traditional hard drives. With speeds reaching up to 1050MB/s for reading and 1000MB/s for writing, the new product offers super-fast backups and immediate storage expansion capabilities. Additionally, it includes enhanced heat dissipation features for improved performance.

news.headline: OCLI Vision adds Dr. Kayla McGuiness, OD, an expert in ocular diseases, to their team, now seeing patients at two locations.
news.summary: OCLI Vision is excited to announce the addition of Dr. Kayla McGuiness, OD, a skilled eye care specialist with expertise in complex ocular diseases. Dr. McGuiness is now available to see new patients at both the Richmond Hill and Tribeca locations. This news comes from Garden City, N.Y. on December 6, 2024.

news.headline: Fed's Goolsbee: With a more speculative asset that lacks an obvious real economy use case, it is hard to see what the real impacts will be.
Annual Claritycon2025 Children's Mental Health Conference on July 16-17, 2025, to offer professional development to 600+ attendees.
news.summary: Every year, Claritycon hosts an event focused on children's mental health, attracting over 600 attendees. The conference offers professional growth opportunities, such as Continuing Education Units (CEUs) and Continuing Nursing Education (CNEs), for educators, therapists, physicians, nurses, social workers, mental health professionals, and community stakeholders. The event delves into both internal and external factors impacting children's mental health.

news.headline: James Gross awarded 2025 Grawemeyer psychology prize for emotion regulation work, showing benefits of early management.
news.summary: A Stanford professor was recognized for their groundbreaking research highlighting the advantages of early emotional regulation. The professor was praised for identifying and explaining various emotional management techniques, and for pioneering the field of emotion regulation. The accolade was received in Louisville, Ky., on Dec. 6, 2024.

news.headline: Gosder Cherilus Foundation marks 15 years of community empowerment, partners with Primitiv Boston to provide toys for families in need.
news.summary: The Gosder Cherilus Foundation (GCF) marks a milestone of 15 years dedicated to supporting disadvantaged communities in locations such as Haiti, Detroit, Somerville, and Boston. As part of their ongoing philanthropic initiatives, GCF collaborates with Primitiv Boston during the festive period to offer toys to families facing hardship. Through partnerships and matched contributions, the foundation continues its mission of giving back and making a positive impact in these areas and beyond.

news.headline: Upanup aids Ontario municipalities in sharing holiday events with residents; web solutions keep them informed.
news.summary: Upanup's web solutions in Victoria, BC, unveiled on Dec. 6, 2024, are aiding municipalities in keeping their residents informed about holiday events and recreational programs. Amid the bustling holiday season, numerous Ontario residents face difficulties in staying updated. The innovative web solutions offer a convenient means of staying informed about events and programs.

news.headline: Fed's Goolsbee: So far, the rise of crypto-based assets has not had much macro impact, but it could have a wealth effect.
CUPE urges Ontario to ban healthcare staffing agencies following new bill requiring data collection.
news.summary: In Toronto, the Canadian Union of Public Employees is pushing for the ban of agency staff in Ontario's healthcare sector, prompted by the introduction of new legislation by the Ford government. The union claims the bill does not effectively tackle the increasing staffing challenges in the industry. Under the proposed legislation, health care staffing agencies are only mandated to submit "aggregate administrative, billing, or pay rate information" to the health minister. The specifics of this submission will be outlined in subsequent regulations.

news.headline: Psyence Group settles obligations with Psyence Biomedical by receiving 337,750 common shares at $3.88 per share.
news.summary: On December 6, 2024, Psyence Group Inc (CSE: PSYG) announced an addendum to its debt-for-equity swap agreements with Psyence Biomedical Ltd (NASDAQ: PBM). According to the agreement, Psyence Biomedical will issue 337,750 common shares to Psyence Group at a price of US$3.88 per share. This transfer will settle the remaining obligations under the Debt Swap Agreements and a promissory note dated January 25, 2024. Psyence Group's associate, Psyence Biomedical, listed its common shares on the NASDAQ in January 2024.

news.headline: Fed's Goolsbee: The Fed will get to 2%, we should not consider changing the target.
Ant International strengthens digital payment ecosystem in 2024, reporting growth in Alipay+, Antom, WorldFirst, and Embedded Finance.
news.summary: In 2024, Ant International, a prominent global digital payment and financial technology provider based in Singapore, experienced significant growth across its four main business divisions - Alipay+, Antom, WorldFirst, and Embedded Finance. Through a series of organizational and strategic enhancements, the company witnessed strong progress. Alipay+ saw expansion in its wallet-based payment and digitalization services, reinforcing the fintech offerings of WorldFirst and Antom. The newly established Embedded Finance segment also contributed to the positive performance.

news.headline: BetterUp and Workday partner to use Workday Peakon Employee Voice to improve coaching and measure transformation impact.
news.summary: In Austin, Texas, BetterUp®, a company specializing in human transformation through virtual coaching, has revealed an enhanced integration with Workday, Inc. (NASDAQ: WDAY), a prominent provider of human capital and financial management solutions. This collaboration will see BetterUp utilizing data from Workday Peakon Employee Voice to enhance the deployment of coaching services, evaluate the effectiveness of transformation programs, and empower clients to enhance engagement levels. This partnership aims to leverage Workday's insights in improving coaching strategies and measuring the outcomes of transformation endeavors for optimal results.

news.headline: US consumer confidence hits highest level since April, reaching new peak.
Landon Smith named President of Executive Title Company, bringing 30 years of industry experience.
news.summary: In Houston, Executive Title Company, a Perry Homes affiliate, has named Landon Smith as its new President. Smith, a veteran in the title insurance, technology, and financial services sectors with 30+ years of experience, is known for his skill in fostering innovation, operational efficiency, and client-centered strategies. Over his career, Smith has effectively introduced and expanded product offerings in real estate segments.

news.headline: Unified Commerce Group closes majority investment in Utah-based retailer Bӧhme, after making an initial investment in early 2024.
news.summary: Today in Salt Lake City, Unified Commerce Group (UCG) revealed the completion of their investment in the Utah-founded women's clothing store Böhme. The initial investment was made in early 2024 and has now been further boosted this month, solidifying UCG's position in the company. The specifics of the investment were not disclosed in the announcement dated December 6, 2024.

news.headline: Halper Sadeh LLC is investigating USAP, BRKH, ARCH, CYTH for securities violations, Dec. 6, 2024.
news.summary: A legal firm, Halper Sadeh LLC, based in New York, has initiated investigations into several companies regarding potential breaches of federal securities laws and fiduciary duties towards shareholders. One of the companies under scrutiny is Universal Stainless & Alloy Products, Inc. This investigation aims to uncover any violations or misconduct for the benefit of shareholders. The firm's efforts are focused on ensuring transparency and accountability in the corporate sector.

news.headline: Fed's Goolsbee: The recent rise in productivity does seem to be concentrated in high-technology spaces, which can have a longer runway.
Bitcoin briefly hits $100,000; Benzinga readers predict it will end 2024 over $100,000. Poll results: Under $90,000: 18%, $90,000-$100,000: 30%, $100,000-$120,000: 40%, over $120,000: 12%.
news.summary: Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) recently surged past the $100,000 milestone following the 2024 presidential election, sparking optimism among investors. The cryptocurrency hit new all-time highs in the wake of Donald Trump's electoral victory. With the year drawing to a close, predictions are rife about Bitcoin's performance by the end of 2024. A recent poll by Benzinga asked readers to forecast Bitcoin's price at year-end: Under $90,000 was predicted by 18%, $90,000 to $100,000 by 30%, $100,000 to $120,000 by 40%, and over $120,000 by 12% of respondents. The majority, 40%, envisioned the price falling within the $100,000 to $120,000 range. Overall, Bitcoin's recent surge and the election results have amplified expectations for its future trajectory.

news.headline: AUO launches 3D Smart Surgical Imaging Platform, part of its smart healthcare push with advanced technologies.
news.summary: AUO is aggressively expanding its efforts to transform its value and enhance its lineup of medical display and high-precision sensing technologies.

news.headline: Vishay's proximity sensor wins China IoT Innovation Award and EE Awards Asia Award, with idle current as low as 5 μA.
news.summary: The VCNL36828P, equipped with a VCSEL and Smart Dual I²C Slave Address, is a compact sensor measuring 2.0 mm x 1.0 mm x 0.5 mm, offering an idle current as low as 5 μA.

news.headline: Linkers Industries Limited closes IPO, sells 1.9M Class A Ordinary Shares at $4 each, trading on Nasdaq as "LNKS".
news.summary: Linkers Industries Limited, a wire/cable harness manufacturer in Malaysia, completed its initial public offering on December 5, 2024. The company, listed on Nasdaq under the symbol “LNKS”, sold 1,900,000 Class A ordinary shares at $4.00 per share, raising a total of $7,600,000 through the offering. This marks an important milestone for the company as it expands its presence and operations in the market.

news.headline: Fed's Goolsbee: Rates will be a fair bit lower a year from now.
US court upholds law requiring TikTok's sale by Jan. 19 if not sold to a non-Chinese firm; ByteDance faces ban.
news.summary: A law in the United States will prohibit the video app, TikTok, from operating by January 19th if its parent company, ByteDance, does not sell it to a non-Chinese entity.

news.headline: Fed's Goolsbee: Given how the rest of the economy is behaving, the one outlier is interest rates, which will likely come down over the next year.
Fed's Goolsbee: The Fed will have a series of meetings that will be close calls between cutting and not cutting.
Fed's Goolsbee: I won't precommit to December meeting decision; still several data points to come including inflation and consumer spending.
Fed's Goolsbee: Overall progress on inflation is still encouraging.
Reworld™ employees support Island Harvest at Bethpage Turkey Drive, Long Island's largest food drive, now in its 16th year.
news.summary: In Bethpage, New York on December 6, 2024, employees from Reworld™ teamed up with Island Harvest Food Bank for the Bethpage Turkey Drive. This event is recognized as Long Island's biggest one-day food drive and took place at Bethpage Federal Credit Union's headquarters. The drive, which marked its 16th year, attracted significant participation from the community.

news.headline: Fed's Goolsbee: Inflation has inched up a bit. I still think the US is on the path to 2%.
HSBC forecasts S&P 500 index target of 6700 for year-end 2025.
GSI Technology to virtually participate in 27th Needham Growth Conference on Jan 17, 2025. CEO & VP to deliver presentation.
news.summary: GSI Technology, Inc., located in Sunnyvale, California, shared news today that they will take part in the 27th Annual Needham Growth Conference on January 17, 2025. The conference will be virtual, and key members from GSI, including Lee-Lean Shu, the Chairman and CEO, and Didier Lasserre, the VP of Sales and Investor Relations, will lead a company presentation at 1:30 p.m. Eastern time on January 17 in Virtual Track 2. Additionally, they will be conducting individual virtual meetings on the same day. GSI Technology is known for creating the Associative Processing Unit (APU), which is considered a groundbreaking development in AI and HPC processing due to its true compute-in-memory technology. The company is listed on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol GSIT.

news.headline: Buy-to-Let mortgage securitization and sale of junior economic stake in PMF 2024-2 plc, involving significant funds.
Illinois Commerce Commission approved a $110 million rate hike for Illinois American Water, disappointing CUB and AARP Illinois.
news.summary: In Chicago on December 6, 2024, it was reported that Illinois American Water did not receive the full rate hike of $110 million they requested, which brought some relief. However, the company still saw a substantial increase in rates. The ICC's decision has emphasized the importance of the Citizens Utility Board's efforts to advocate for water reform in the state.

news.headline: Thomson Reuters named Leader in 2024 IDC MarketScape for SaaS Sales and Use Tax Automation Software.
news.summary: On December 6, 2024, Thomson Reuters, a leading global content and technology company listed on the NYSE and TSX under the symbol TRI, revealed its recognition as a Leader in two IDC MarketScape reports. The reports titled "Worldwide SaaS" and "Cloud-Enabled SaaS Sales and Use Tax Automation" have acknowledged Thomson Reuters for its exceptional performance in these areas. This achievement highlights the company's strength and expertise in the industry.

news.headline: Over 30 crypto artists unite for charity auction benefiting St. Jude Research Hospital, supporting children's cancer research.
news.summary: Motivated by a personal loss, Leap, a prominent figure in the crypto world, has gathered a group of more than 30 artists to participate in a charity auction. The proceeds from this event will go towards supporting the St. Jude Children's Research Hospital.

news.headline: Fed's Goolsbee: What happens with immigration will have a very significant impact on the size of the coming labor force.
